word: The evidence was abundantly clear.
definition: in large quantities; plentifully

word: The weather forecast was bad, and we dressed accordingly.
definition: in a way that is appropriate

word: The new policy was implemented administratively without a vote.
definition: in an administrative manner

word: He ran afoul of the law.
definition: In conflict or difficulty with

word: He allegedly committed the crime.
definition: allegedly

word: The flag waved proudly aloft.
definition: up in the air; overhead

word: Apropos of your earlier comment, I've found some new information.
definition: with reference to; concerning (often used to introduce a relevant topic)

word: He assuredly knew the answer to the question.
definition: certainly; without a doubt

word: The old book fell asunder in his hands.
definition: into pieces

word: Biologically, humans are very similar to other primates.
definition: in a biological way; from a biological point of view.

word: He categorically denied the accusations.
definition: absolutely; without qualification

word: The patient's ability to process information cognitively was impaired.
definition: In a way that relates to cognition; mentally.

word: The problem is computationally intensive, requiring powerful computers.
definition: In a way that relates to or involves computation.

word: Conceptually, the plan was sound, but practical implementation was difficult.
definition: In terms of concepts or ideas.

word: The new law was deemed constitutionally sound.
definition: in a way that is allowed or restricted by a constitution

word: The new policy was demonstrably effective in reducing crime.
definition: in a way that can be clearly shown or proved

word: Their opinions were diametrically opposed on the issue.
definition: completely; directly opposite

word: The rich disproportionately benefit from these tax cuts.
definition: to an extent that is too large or too small in comparison with something else

word: The documents were duly signed and submitted.
definition: in accordance with what is required or appropriate; properly

word: The theory was empirically tested through experiments.
definition: by means of observation or experience rather than theory or pure logic

word: The car was swerving erratically down the road.
definition: in an unpredictable or irregular way

word: He erroneously believed the train would arrive on time.
definition: in a mistaken or incorrect way

word: She vowed to love him evermore.
definition: forever; always

word: The task was exceedingly difficult.
definition: to an unusually great degree; extremely

word: The rules were explicitly stated in the contract.
definition: in a clear and detailed manner, leaving no room for confusion or doubt

word: The rules were expressly designed to prevent cheating.
definition: for a particular purpose; explicitly

word: The painting was exquisitely detailed.
definition: beautifully

word: The police made a forcible entry into the building.
definition: done by force

word: The order must be carried out forthwith.
definition: immediately; without delay

word: He gave a grudging apology, clearly not meaning it.
definition: given or done unwillingly (adjective)

word: He rushed headlong into the crowd.
definition: with the head foremost; in a rush

word: Henceforth, all employees must wear their ID badges.
definition: from this time forward

word: Hereafter, all meetings will begin promptly at 9 AM.
definition: from this time on or in the future; in the afterlife

word: I hereby declare the meeting open.
definition: by this means; as a result of this

word: Hitherto, the project had been running smoothly.
definition: until now or until the point in time under discussion

word: The two parties were ideologically opposed on many issues.
definition: in a way that relates to or is based on ideology

word: His inadvertent remark caused some offense.
definition: not resulting from or achieved through deliberate planning

word: He is indisputably the best player on the team.
definition: in a way that cannot be challenged or denied

word: The inexorable march of time affects us all.
definition: impossible to stop or prevent

word: The lights went out inexplicably.
definition: in a way that cannot be explained

word: Their fates were inextricably linked.
definition: in a way that is impossible to disentangle or separate

word: Insofar as I understand the situation, we are making good progress.
definition: to the extent that

word: The light came on instantaneously when she flipped the switch.
definition: at once; instantly

word: His reaction was purely instinctive.
definition: based on instinct, automatic

word: The terms 'happy' and 'joyful' are often used interchangeably.
definition: in a way that can be exchanged without making a difference

word: His invariable good humor was a comfort to everyone.
definition: never changing; constant

word: The decision was irrevocable.
definition: not able to be changed, reversed, or recovered

word: Latterly, he has shown great improvement in his studies.
definition: recently; in latter times

word: Her performance has improved markedly since last year.
definition: noticeably

word: The artist masterfully blended the colors in the painting.
definition: in a very skillful or expert way

word: The new policy will materially affect our profits.
definition: Significantly; substantially

word: He was morbidly fascinated by true crime stories.
definition: in a morbid manner

word: There was nary a sound in the empty house.
definition: not a single; no

word: He is nominally in charge, but others make the decisions.
definition: in name only; theoretically

word: It was a difficult task, but he succeeded nonetheless.
definition: in spite of that; nevertheless

word: Notwithstanding the bad weather, the event was a success.
definition: in spite of; nevertheless

word: He obliquely hinted at the problem without directly addressing it.
definition: not in a direct way; indirectly

word: He resigned ostensibly due to health reasons, but many suspected otherwise.
definition: as appears or is stated to be true, though not necessarily so

word: He overtly expressed his disapproval of the plan.
definition: openly, not secretly

word: He painstakingly restored the antique clock to its original condition.
definition: with great care and thoroughness

word: His excuse was patently false; no one believed him.
definition: Clearly; obviously.

word: The old clock in the hall perpetually chimed every hour.
definition: continuously, endlessly

word: The new product sold phenomenally well in its first month.
definition: exceptionally, remarkably

word: The body reacts physiologically to stress by releasing hormones.
definition: in a physiological manner

word: He argued his case quite plausibly, despite the lack of concrete evidence.
definition: in a way that seems reasonable or probable

word: We will preferentially select candidates with relevant experience.
definition: in a way that shows preference

word: He offered his profuse apologies for the mistake.
definition: abundant; flowing freely

word: The cost of the new software was prohibitively expensive for small businesses.
definition: to an extent that prevents something from being done or achieved

word: The experience affected him psychologically.
definition: in a psychological manner; relating to the mind

word: The purported leader of the group denied the accusations.
definition: appearing or stated to be true, though not necessarily so; allegedly

word: The new system is qualitatively better than the old one.
definition: in terms of quality or kind rather than quantity

word: He is reputedly the best chef in the city.
definition: according to what is generally said or believed

word: The new policy will apply retroactively to the beginning of the year.
definition: with effect from a date in the past

word: He was roundly criticized for his actions.
definition: in a thorough or emphatic manner

word: He ruthlessly pursued his goals.
definition: without pity or compassion

word: She was scantily dressed for the cold weather.
definition: in a scanty or meager way; barely

word: She was singularly talented among her peers.
definition: in a singular or unique way; exceptionally

word: The truth was starkly revealed in the report.
definition: in a severe or bare manner

word: Her new painting was strikingly beautiful.
definition: In a way that is very noticeable or impressive.

word: The view from the mountain was stunningly beautiful.
definition: in an extremely impressive or attractive manner

word: He subconsciously tapped his foot during the meeting.
definition: without conscious awareness

word: The cost has increased substantially.
definition: to a great extent

word: The employee was summarily dismissed for misconduct.
definition: in a summary manner; without delay or formality

word: They had a surreptitious meeting in the park.
definition: kept secret, especially because it would not be approved of

word: He moved to London in 2005 and lived there thereafter.
definition: after that

word: He failed the exam, thereby losing his scholarship.
definition: by that means

word: The contract outlines the terms, and therein lies the problem.
definition: in that place, document, or respect

word: He knocked thrice before entering the room.
definition: three times

word: She stated her opinion unapologetically.
definition: in a way that shows no regret or apology

word: Unbeknownst to him, his friends were planning a surprise party.
definition: without the knowledge of (someone)

word: She loved him unconditionally.
definition: without conditions or limitations

word: He is undeniably one of the best players in the league.
definition: in a way that cannot be denied

word: Don't be unduly concerned about the minor delay.
definition: to an unwarranted or excessive degree

word: She stated unequivocally that she would not support the proposal.
definition: in a way that leaves no doubt; unambiguously.

word: The company decided to unilaterally change the terms of the contract.
definition: done by one person or group without the agreement of others

word: She was unquestionably the best candidate for the job.
definition: beyond doubt

word: She unwittingly revealed the secret during the conversation.
definition: without being aware; unintentionally

word: The company is moving upwardly in the market.
definition: in an upward direction or position

word: She was utterly exhausted after the long journey.
definition: completely and without qualification

word: The small army fought valiantly against overwhelming odds.
definition: with courage or determination

word: The problem has been variously described as a crisis or a challenge.
definition: in different ways or at different times

word: She repeated his instructions verbatim to ensure accuracy.
definition: in exactly the same words as were used originally

word: She felt a vicarious thrill watching the extreme sports.
definition: experienced in the imagination through the feelings or actions of another person

word: The contract specifies the terms wherein the agreement is valid.
definition: in what place or respect; in which

word: The decision was wholly unexpected.
definition: entirely; fully

word: His woeful expression showed how disappointed he was.
definition: full of woe; sorrowful

word: Look at the beautiful mountains over yonder.
definition: at some distance in the direction indicated
